description:
You are a deployment-automation engineer.  
    Two raw application logs already exist under the directory  
    /home/user/release-logs/raw/ :  

        • /home/user/release-logs/raw/app1.log  
        • /home/user/release-logs/raw/app2.log  
      
    1.  Extract every line that contains the literal string “ERROR” from each raw log,  
        mask every IPv4 address in those lines by replacing it with the four-octet token  
        “X.X.X.X”, and save the result in two new files located in  
        /home/user/release-logs/processed/ :  
            a) /home/user/release-logs/processed/error_app1.log  
            b) /home/user/release-logs/processed/error_app2.log  

        The text of each new file must be identical to its source line-for-line, except that:  
           • only lines that originally contained “ERROR” are kept;  
           • every IPv4 address is swapped for the exact string “X.X.X.X”.  
        Do NOT reorder the lines inside each file.  

    2.  Build an aggregated chronological report named  
        /home/user/release-logs/error_report.txt with the exact format below:  

            Line-1 :  the literal text  “ERROR REPORT GENERATED” (without quotes)  
            Line-2 :  a single, completely blank line  
            Line-3+ : one line per error, sorted in ascending chronological order  
                      across BOTH logs.  The format of each of these lines is  
                      <timestamp>|<source>|<message>,  where  
                         • <timestamp> is the full timestamp that appears at the  
                           beginning of the original log line,  
                         • <source>   is “app1” or “app2”,  
                         • <message> is everything that followed the substring  
                           “ERROR ” in the original line (same spacing, no leading/trailing
                           blanks, and with any IPv4 address already masked).  

        Example (spacing and punctuation are significant):  
            2023-12-01 08:16:12,456|app1|Failed to load resource /api/data  

    3.  Produce a CSV summary named /home/user/release-logs/summary.csv.  
        It must obey ALL of the following rules so that automatic grading can parse it:  
           • The file is plain text, UTF-8, Unix line endings.  
           • The very first line is the header exactly:  
                 source,total_errors,first_seen,last_seen  
           • For each application (app1, app2) output exactly one data line using those  
             four comma-separated columns:  
                 – source        : the literal word app1 or app2  
                 – total_errors  : integer count of “ERROR” lines for that app  
                 – first_seen    : the earliest timestamp among its errors  
                 – last_seen     : the latest  timestamp among its errors  
           • Do not surround fields with quotes, do not add extra spaces.  
           • The order of the two data lines does not matter.  

    4.  Create one more file, /home/user/release-logs/processed/sanitized_all.log, that  
        contains every single line from BOTH raw logs (that is, not just the errors) with  
        every IPv4 address masked to “X.X.X.X”.  Preserve the original order: all lines from  
        app1.log first, then all lines from app2.log.  

    Constraints & hints for the internal implementation (they are NOT verified directly,  
    but following them will make the task easier):  
        • Use awk and/or sed as your primary processing tools; feel free to combine them  
          with standard utilities like sort, uniq, tee, cut, etc.  
        • The agent will not receive root privileges; stay inside /home/user.  
        • Create the directory /home/user/release-logs/processed/ if it does not exist.  
        • The automated grader will compare your output files byte-for-byte with the  
          expected solution, so mind every letter, delimiter and newline.  
        • IPv4 addresses always follow the pattern “d.d.d.d” where d is 1-3 digits.
truth:
Initial directory tree prior to the agent’s first command:
        /home/user/release-logs/
            └── raw/
                ├── app1.log
                └── app2.log

    Contents of the existing raw logs:

    /home/user/release-logs/raw/app1.log
    ------------------------------------
    2023-12-01 08:15:42,123 INFO User 192.168.1.10 logged in
    2023-12-01 08:16:12,456 ERROR Failed to load resource /api/data
    2023-12-01 08:17:05,789 WARN Cache miss for user 42
    2023-12-01 09:03:22,105 ERROR Database timeout after 30s
    2023-12-01 09:05:01,333 INFO User 192.168.1.15 logged out

    /home/user/release-logs/raw/app2.log
    ------------------------------------
    2023-12-01 08:20:11,111 INFO Deployment started by user devops
    2023-12-01 08:21:45,222 ERROR Service xyz failed health-check
    2023-12-01 08:23:30,555 INFO Service xyz restarted successfully
    2023-12-01 08:24:18,777 ERROR Could not connect to redis at 10.0.0.5:6379
    2023-12-01 08:25:55,999 INFO Deployment finished in 344s


    Files that must exist when the agent finishes (and their exact required content):

    1) /home/user/release-logs/processed/error_app1.log
    ---------------------------------------------------
    2023-12-01 08:16:12,456 ERROR Failed to load resource /api/data
    2023-12-01 09:03:22,105 ERROR Database timeout after 30s

    2) /home/user/release-logs/processed/error_app2.log
    ---------------------------------------------------
    2023-12-01 08:21:45,222 ERROR Service xyz failed health-check
    2023-12-01 08:24:18,777 ERROR Could not connect to redis at X.X.X.X:6379

    3) /home/user/release-logs/error_report.txt
    -------------------------------------------
    ERROR REPORT GENERATED

    2023-12-01 08:16:12,456|app1|Failed to load resource /api/data
    2023-12-01 08:21:45,222|app2|Service xyz failed health-check
    2023-12-01 08:24:18,777|app2|Could not connect to redis at X.X.X.X:6379
    2023-12-01 09:03:22,105|app1|Database timeout after 30s

    4) /home/user/release-logs/summary.csv
    --------------------------------------
    source,total_errors,first_seen,last_seen
    app1,2,2023-12-01 08:16:12,456,2023-12-01 09:03:22,105
    app2,2,2023-12-01 08:21:45,222,2023-12-01 08:24:18,777

    5) /home/user/release-logs/processed/sanitized_all.log
    ------------------------------------------------------
    2023-12-01 08:15:42,123 INFO User X.X.X.X logged in
    2023-12-01 08:16:12,456 ERROR Failed to load resource /api/data
    2023-12-01 08:17:05,789 WARN Cache miss for user 42
    2023-12-01 09:03:22,105 ERROR Database timeout after 30s
    2023-12-01 09:05:01,333 INFO User X.X.X.X logged out
    2023-12-01 08:20:11,111 INFO Deployment started by user devops
    2023-12-01 08:21:45,222 ERROR Service xyz failed health-check
    2023-12-01 08:23:30,555 INFO Service xyz restarted successfully
    2023-12-01 08:24:18,777 ERROR Could not connect to redis at X.X.X.X:6379
    2023-12-01 08:25:55,999 INFO Deployment finished in 344s
